topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box sat on the worn welcome mat like a monument to forgotten chores. Mark stared at it, his gaze drifting from the mundane peeling paint on the wall to the brown parcel resting beside it. Another delivery, another thing to deal with. A sigh escaped him, a deep, drawn-out sound that seemed to vibrate the stillness of the apartment. He didn't move. His limbs felt heavy, anchored to the sofa cushions. He contemplated the sheer effort required to open it, to sort through whatever contents were inside. The thought of bending over, of engaging in any kind of physical exertion, was simply too much to process right now. He decided, with the firm resolve of a stone, to leave it there until tomorrow, or perhaps never.

[story 2]

When the doorbell rang, Sarah barely registered the sound. She was draped across the armchair, a half-finished book resting precariously on her chest, her eyes glazed over with a pleasant vacancy. A delivery person stood awkwardly by the door, holding a package addressed to the former resident. Sarah blinked slowly, a deliberate, sluggish movement. She didn't rise. The package seemed to demand attention, but the energy required to pivot, to retrieve the item, felt monumental. A faint, warm pressure settled in her shoulders, a signal that movement was unwelcome. "Oh, well," she mumbled, her voice thick with inertia, "that can wait." She settled back into the soft embrace of the upholstery, effectively conceding the item's existence to the next available moment.

[story 3]

Liam shuffled into the hallway, his steps slow and deliberate, as if wading through thick syrup. The package, clearly marked for someone else, caught his eye. He stopped just short of picking it up, his posture slumping further into the frame. The simple act of lifting the box felt like an insurmountable obstacle course. His mind offered no immediate motivation to engage; the world outside the confines of his current state seemed distant and unimportant. He leaned against the doorframe, letting his weight settle against the wood, wishing intensely for the floor to simply rise up and meet him. The package remained untouched, a silent testament to the sheer unwillingness to participate in anything requiring exertion.
